paragraphs.text: Hej for the European Commission. I can tell you with certainty that I am grateful for the opportunity you have given us with this initiative. The digital market is undoubtedly very complex, but unfortunately most European citizens are equally aware of it. With this in mind, most of the European population is at risk of being exploited by “grey” companies. Companies publishing virtual products, such as software, including creativity tools, such as Adobe Creative Cloud, where customers are required to pay 50 % of the remaining amount of their annual subscription if they want to cancel the subscription even if they have not had it for 12 months. The Federal Trade Commission in the United States voted in favour of referring the civil penalty complaint to their Department of Justice, which lodged the complaint with the US District Court for the Northern District of California. The complaint was based on the fact that Adobe’s termination terms are saved out on the company’s website with small size or require consumers to keep the mouse over small icons to find the information. Companies publishing virtual products, such as online games, where customers lose access to their product because companies cannot predict economic benefits if they were to continue to maintain game servers and periodic updates. A popular example was created by the company, Ubisoft, where the online race game ‘The Crew’ had its servers shut down, from which customers could not play the game at all, even though most of the entertainment could well be enjoyed offline. I invite you to take these concerns seriously and consider stricter regulations to protect European consumers from unfair business practices in the digital market. Transparency, fair subscription terms and the consumer’s right to own and use purchased products without undue restrictions should be the focus. Thank you for your attention and work to ensure a fairer digital market for all. With your pleasure, A worried citizen.
